changelog.text: Updates for data pad update, Dux, Condor, and bot re-evaluations: Dagon -1 Longevity, Ophion +1 Lethality, Shenlou +1 Accessibility, Dux added at total +1, Condor added at total +1. - Mistermath
Updates for data pad update: Pathfinder +2 Accessibility - Mistermath
Update for 10.6 patch: Ravager added at total 0. - Mistermath
Added and began work on Camelot ratings. - Mistermath
Completed Camelot tier list. - Mistermath
Added Samjok at total +1. - Mistermath
Updates for data pad update: Curie -1 Accessibility, Raptor +1 Accessibility, Bagliore +1 Accessibility. - Mistermath
Moved Doc from F-tier to C-tier on the SAGE Retro Camelot tier list. - Mistermath
Bot re-evaluations: Strider -1 Lethality, Mars -1 Longevity, Blitz +1 Accessibility. - Mistermath
Bot re-evaluations: Harpy -1 Lethality. - Mistermath

Bot re-shifts owing to 10.8 patch. Related re-evaluations: Invader +1 Accessibility, Blitz +1 Accessibility, Leech +1 Mobility, Phantom +1 Accessibility & -1 Durability, Ao Jun +1 Accessibility. - Mistermath
Update for 10.8 patch: Condor -1 Lethality & -1 Mobility, Dux -1 Mobility, Raptor +1 Utility & -1 Accessibility, Nemesis +1 Accessibility & -1 Longevity. - Mistermath
Updates for BM update: Curie +1 Accessibility, Condor +1 Accessibility - Mistermath
Added Lio and Nuo at total +1. - Mistermath
Update for Workshop update: Imugi +2 Accessibility. - Mistermath
Bot re-evaluations: Nether -1 Accessibility. - Mistermath
Bot re-evaluations: Typhon -1 Longevity, Erebus -1 Longevity, Invader -1 Longevity, Mars -1 Accessibility - Mistermath
Bot re-evaluations: Hellburner -1 Accessibility. - Mistermath
Updates for data pad update: Dux +2 Accessibility, Ravager +1 Accessibility. Added Sword Unit at total 0. - Mistermath
Corrected a data entry error for Typhon and Nether. - Mistermath
Added Teth at a total of +1 - MrCactus
11.2 Mega Patch updates for a total of: Orochi +1, Condor -1, Dux +1,  Samjok +1, Nuo +2 - MrCactus
Modified changes made on 07/01 following review; Orochi +1 Longevity reverted to 0 Longevity. Condor's -1 Accessibility kept, added -1 Longevity. - Mistermath
Added Strix at a total of -1 - MrCactus
Bot re-evaluations: Revenant 0 Accessibility, Ochokotchi 0 Accessibility, Raptor 0 Accessibility. - MrCactus

Bot re-evaluations: Scorpion 2, Shenlou 1, Ao Jun -1, Condor -1, Loki 2, Pathfinder 5, Lio/Nuo 3, Demeter 3, Dux 2, Crisis 2. Changed rating system to allow -2 and +2 in certain traits, allowing more precise control of tier by extensuating a bots extreme proficiency (or lack thereof) in any given trait. Added Kaji, Rex, Hastatus -Adazahi
Bot re-evaluations: Tyr, Fenrir, Weyland, Behemoth -1, Ophion 0, Fury, Orochi 1, Dagon -2. Strix changed to Stryx - Adazahi
Bot re-eval: Crisis access -1. Four good f2p heavy weapons? In this economy? - Adazahi
Added experimental layout for a titan tab. - Mistermath

Bedwyr 0 lethality (+1) due to deathmark. Princeps +1 lethality +1 longevity and +1 utility due to it being damage dealer with TNA and 2 alpha 2 beta as well as the force push ability that can CC enemies. -3 accessibility due to it being paid gambling only, so overall same grade. Atlas utility +1 mobility +1 due to jump, accessibility -2 for same reason. 
Bjorn +1 lethality, it has an entire alpha weapon on most brawlers. Added Kid, Ming, Arthur. Kaji 0 longevity, nondens 0 longevity. -Adazahi

After a bit of discussion through discord, Crisis deserves +1 mobility if Bagliore has +1 for having a simple relatively one-time use beacon teleport (which should be used defensively instead of for covering ground, but that's neither here nor there) -Tropical
Skyros +2 mobility -1 lethality to be consistent with Loki -Adazahi changed adazahi password and signed him out of his account have fun 😂-tropical
Blitz utility +1, access 0, since it has suppression but still relies on statchecking when the suppression runs out (or if the enemy has anticontrol). also, blew up tropical's house. -adzadongy

Bot re-evaluations: Crisis mobility +1->0, Bagliore mobility +1->0, accessibility -2->-1, Ravager accessibility +1->0, Dux Longevity 0->+1 Mobility +1->0.
Footnote: Considering re-evaluation of accessibility ratings for Sword-Unit and Teth. Let this also be a reminder that the point of rating things systematically is that the process should determine the results, not the other way around.
Mobility is most certainly not a category Crisis excels in. Lethality and Accessibility should be the two areas where a debate can be had. 
An argument that either Flux/Gauss Crisis is a legitimate build or that Crisis should have +2 Lethality would be still lukewarm, but significantly more tenable than Crisis being a mobility king. - Mistermath
Math and I decided on Crisis, Stryx, and Bagliore all sharing +2 lethality as they are the three highest firepower piercer-holders in the game, and unlike behemoth, the only other 4H bot, also have either damage boosts or 5H. -Adazahi
Bot re-evaluations: Teth & Sword Unit Accessibility -1 -> 0. - Mistermath

Bot re-evaluations: Blitz Utility +1->0. Moved back down to bring in line with other suppression robots. Consider raising Rayker from 0 to +1 in the future due to significantly more range and charges on suppression. - Mistermath
Bot re-evaluations: Ravager Longevity 0->+1. Bringing in line with Loki and Crisis. Worth noting that Ravager's superior bulk to Crisis may allow it to act as a slower Loki. -Mistermath

Bot re-evaluations: Fury Utility +1->-1, Accessibility +1->+2. Jaeger mobility -1->0. Changes for the purposes of consistency. In terms of accessibility, Fury is the only pre-leveled T3 bot. -Mistermath
Added Ammit at a total rating of +2. -adazi
Changed sword description to remove anything about execute. It's execution threshold is too low to reliably execute things anymore - Adazahi
Added new "Bot Roles" page. -Mistermath & Adazahi

Edited the Nuo & Lio short blurbs to exclude the "available through common data pads" part, and increased Hastatus's accessibility rating to -1 from -2. At least, with Kaji being at -1 and 
Hastatus also being in the same pads & locations as Kaji, I think it's fair to have it at -1. Also somewhat of the same reasoning as to why Bagliore has -1 in accessibility. 
Maybe considering increasing Rex's accessibility to -2 from -3? My reasoning being Bull isn't mandatory (personally ran it long enough to determine that mk3 is the bigger & more impactful hurdle), 
and the fact Ammit h-as -2 despite being economically the same as Rex was on its release. -Tropical

Edited Nuo & Lio comments to mention expected future additions to superchest in order to justify maintaining accessibility rating. Fixed what appeared to be a consistency error for Orochi and Nether on T4 tab. - Mistermath
Started progress on mini build guides. Added info about roles and mini build guides section to the intro. - Adazahi
Finished mini build guide. -adazahi
Added hastatus build guide, removed titan chargers, devourer 0->+1 utility and +1->0 longevity, edited descriptions for Bjorn, Devourer, and Princeps - Adazahi
Removed ARM from pathfinder build guide. % damage weapons dont scale well with hunter stacks. Added pretty colors for organization. -Adazahi
Update for 11.8 patch: Lio longevity +2->+1. Corrected utility for Rex/Hastatus from 0->-1. -Mistermath
Added Anaskor, gradually working on rating changes for other Robots due to rebalance wave. -Tropical

Ankasor lethality +2 -> +1. While Ankasor sports decent firepower and a really good BiW, it's seemingly insane firepower is carried by the OP new guns, which the other +2 Lethality bots (ie Bagliore) can wield many more of. -Adazahi

Kaji mobility 1 -> 0, Access -1 -> 0. Nerfs really reduced it's speed, but it's in common data pads now. Rex Longevity 2 -> 1, Access -3 -> 2. Rex isn't as tanky as before, but it's a little easier to get your hands on now. 
Princeps access -3 -> -2, Atlas access -2 -> -1, both are & have been inside the titan pads for a few months now. Sword mobility 1 -> 0, don't use the dash anymore, utility -1 -> 0, run it like a siren with lockdown unit. 
Updated a few robot & titan comments, roles, and mini build guides as well. -Adazahi & Tropical
Re-evaluated what counts as 2 mobility. Imugi, Nightingale, Strider, Nuo, Raven, Nether, and Seraph all have been raised from 1 -> 2. -Adazahi
Changed pilot recommendation on Skirmisher Anaksor to Mei Lan -Adazahi

Added UE Devastator + UE Havoc to Anaksor titan slayer build suggestions. Eiffel and Bersagliere Mobility changed from 1 -> 2 to fit with new mobility evaluation standards. Dagon access changed from -2 -> -1 because while it requires 6 weapons and statchecks, it's available through black market. Stryx, who is available through only data pads and still requires 5 weapons is only -1, so this seems fair. -Adazahi
Changed f2p Imugi mini build guide reccomendation to Growler Howler from Havoc Scatter. -Adazahi

Changed mini build guide weapons: Dux best -> replaced any ue weapons with close range only, Demeter best -> replaced any UE weapons with close range only, Lio f2p -> Growler, Leech f2p -> growler + porthos, 
Nuo best -> Machiara + Basilus or UE redeemer/taran, Lynx f2p -> Nucleon or Talon, Siren -> renamed "titan charger" to "titan charger/assault", Raptor best -> close range UE or kroko guns, Nightingale best -> Elox Murix, 
UE burst weapons, Kroko guns, Teth best -> removed hybrid acid shotgun build, Ammit -> Added machiara + velios and close range UE to best, rust rocket, bsg, and growler to f2p, Raven best -> added midrange UE setups, Anaksor f2p -> added screamer growler
re-added pilum + gladius to all midrange setups that had it entirely replaced with anaksor guns, the extra 100m of range makes it a decent option (though still mostly worse than anaksor guns, still best at 600m). Added UE index, added rust machine guns to any bots with atomizer or nucleon suggested.
-Adazahi

Re-evaluations: Samjok Access 0 -> +1, Anaksor Access -2 -> -3. Samjok is in the gold chest and doesn't require much leveling to function. Anaksor without the drone has issues with chip damage. -Adazahi
Added Vector & Waymaker. -Tropical. Added Vector to mini build guides + edited anaksor best pilot to be the new one. -Adazahi
Added new curving flames to mini build guides. -Adazahi Fixed the way adazahi credits his notes in the changes log -Tropical
Re-evaluations: Bulgasari mobility 0 -> +1, Haechi mobility 0 -> +1. Multiple dashes are now sufficient for +1 rating. Rex accessibility -2 -> -1 following move to common/blue pads. -Mistermath
Changed F2P builds for Loki and Skyros (beacon capper) to harpoons. Non F2P unchanged as meta weapons for the occasional opportunistic attack are probably just as relevant.
Atlas accessibility -1 -> 0 due to being added to the black market and it likely already deserved the 0-rating anyways. - Tropical
Added seeker as an option for dux. -adazahi

Aether longevity -1 -> 0, the forcefield is indeed real protection that requires concentrated firepower from several TNA titans to actually kill through. Fixed atlas being 2 on titan sheet but 3 on intro sheet. 
Murometz mobility 0 -> -1, there isn't any reason for it to have 0 in mobility when it lacks any mobility at all, unless vertical mobility is counted, but then ao ming would have 0 mobility. 
Eiffel lethality 1 ->2, having ran it myself, I feel confident in suggesting eiffel as a final bot for f2ps -Adazahi
Added Myia to Loki, Skyros, and Nightingale recommendations. -Adazahi

Added Shoggoth, Hastatus accessiblity from -1 to 0 because it is now in common data pads, Fixed Nether rating as it was different in some places, Rex lethality 0->1 due to BIW's bug fix, could even argue Rex being potential +2 lethality but that's less about a noob guide and more for power level raking -Tropical

Added shoggoth to mini build guides. May soon be worth considering Piercer to be a f2p weapon. Removed piercer from most "best builds" in favor or Shantak. Added Voonith + Urhag to the best options for most midranger builds.
Yes, I know they have longer range, but I'm not gonna make a new sniper node for every single bot that can use these op ranged guns, they work at midrange too. 
Shoggoth speed 0-> -1, bots with no mobility have -1, not 0. -Adazahi
Added Aopo as a f2p option for Anaksor. -adazahi

builds.best_weapons:
Kroko, Fahrenheit,
UE Cryo, or close
range UE setups
builds.specialization:
Support (Accelerator +
Last Stand)
Repair or UC
builds.explanation:
A high-powered support build with close ranged weapons for self defense against rushing
brawlers. UE Cryo provides more DP through Freezo. Not recommended for f2p.

builds.f2p_drones: Lv9 Freezo
builds.best_weapons:
Midrange UE setups,
Machiara, UE Vortex
Urhag
builds.specialization:
Support (Accelerator +
Last Stand)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
A more versatile support build that's a lot more accessible for f2p players. Can't defend itself
from rushing brawlers, requiring friendlies to protect it, but is able to pick off enemies at range.

builds.best_weapons:
Midrange UE setups,
Machiara, UE Vortex
builds.specialization:
Support (Accelerator +
Last Stand)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
An incredibly niche build that makes use of Lv12 Qingting's lockdown extension to displace
valuable targets (ie meta titans) for a long time. Very stong in very few rare situations, but
trading off Freezo is incredibly hard to justify. Not recommended unless you already have several
built hangars.

builds.best_weapons:
UE Igniter + UE Blaze
(or UE Shredder)
UE Arms
Machiara + Velos
Fahrenheit + Celsius
builds.specialization:
Support (Accelerator +
Last Stand)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Monique's ability allows Imugi to be one of three bots in the game that can trigger Webby's
Rust ability. The rust is strong, but the build won't work without Lv12 Webby. Not recommended for
f2p.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Armadillo
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Close range UE setups,
Fahrenheit + Celsius,
or Elox + Murix
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Kestrel
Lv12 Scales
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Support (Accelerator +
Last Stand)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Mao Ling combined with a defensive drone turn Imugi into an uncharacteristically tanky
robot even when on the ground, allowing it to somewhat sustain a fight even out of ability.

builds.f2p_weapons:
Hazard + Decay
Subduer + Damper,
or Nucleon + Atomizer
builds.f2p_drones:
Lv9 Aopo
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Machiara + Basilius or
UE Redeemer + UE Taran
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Repair, UC, or
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Nuo is able to emulalte Pathfider's OP damage buffs with a bit of a sidegrade. The max
damage boost is lower and requires you to stay alive and linked, but it also stacks MUCH
faster, provides stacks of counterblind, and provides grey immunity + healing with Walter.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv9 Aopo
Lv4 Showdown
Lv4+ Solar
builds.best_weapons: Shantak + Urhag
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Repair, UC, or
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Nuo is probably the only bot in the game that can make a f2p friendly sniper build. Since it gives itself
such a large damage boost that stacks multiplicatively with the defense ignore on Gauss + Weber,
their mediocre performance is somewhat alleviated vs bots relying on DP. Playing at a super long
distance means you're often not linked, so Basil becomes a competitive pilot choice. For f2p, it's
not worth spending memorium on Basil since he's so incredibly niche, and sometimes there
is a friendly sniper in the backline with you that makes great use of the link. For p2w ranged
weapons, Solar becomes the best drone. Solar still works with railguns, but it's value is questionable.

builds.f2p_drones: Lv4 Showdown
builds.best_weapons:
Any UE weapons, Kroko,
Fahrenheit
builds.specialization:
Support (Accelerator +
Last Stand)
Brawler (Repair Amp +
Immune Amp)
Repair or UC
builds.explanation:
While Lio is not nearly as tanky as it once was, it can still share a noticable amount of defense points
with an ally, heal them, and give them grey resistance + occasional countersuppression charges.
Lio should be played conservatively, hiding from high-damage enemies while staying in range of a 
tanky teammate that it's linked to.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Close Range UE weapons,
Kroko, Machiara or Murix
builds.best_drones:
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Support (Accelerator +
Last Stand)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Demeter with Mogwans, Whiteout, and Appmeter provides not only healing and shielding, but
also debuffing to the enemy. At p2w investment, it's better to use OP weapons of your own
rather than Mogwans, though Whiteout is difficult to justify replacing with much other than UE
Glider, which can help you slip away to cover after your shield drops.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv9 Armadillo
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
UE Burst weapons, Kroko,
or Murix
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv12 Armadillo
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Brawler (Repair Amp +
Immune Amp)
Repair or UC
builds.explanation:
Ravana should not attempt to statcheck meta brawlers; rather, its value lies in its ability to
outlast the protective abilities of meta hit-and-run type bots.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv1 Whiteout
Lv1 Myia
builds.best_weapons: Doesn't matter
builds.specialization:
Saboteur (Beacon Op +
Accelerator)
Defense (Anticontrol +
Heavy Armor Kit)
Repair or UC
builds.explanation:
Skyros played as a beacon capper should not leave ball mode. Just keep on rollin' and know
when to eject into a titan.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv1 Whiteout
Lv1 Myia
builds.best_weapons:
Liodari + Kroko + Hippo,
Elox + Murix + UE Glory
builds.specialization:
Saboteur (Beacon Op +
Accelerator)
Defense (Anticontrol +
Heavy Armor Kit)
Repair or UC
builds.explanation:
The scales drone makes the hit-and-run skyros playstyle kind of real, since it means you don't
need theseus for DMI anymore. However, the burst required for this playstyle to be successful
(and not to mention the OP drone) make this a very exclusive build. Not recommended for f2p.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv9 Armadillo
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv9 Kestrel
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Basilieus, Liodari, Kelvin, 
or UE Redeemer
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv12 Armadillo
Lv12 Kestrel
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Any Specialization
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Lynx is currently the only non-UE bot in the game with a reliable execute (Sword's sucks now).
This allows it to be competent at killing bots way out of it's weight class, especially with the aid
of a teammate.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv1 Hawkeye
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Reviver
builds.best_weapons: Athos + Aramis
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Hawkeye
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv12 Reviver
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Phase Shift,
Repair, or UC
builds.explanation:
With the nerf to electric titan charge, Siren is now one of the best chargers, but it only
works if you fire at an enemy with a reflector (so, no Raptors = no charge). It's also signficantly
enhanced when they fire at you. While this build can charge faster than prime electric builds,
it's hindered by the fact that it relies on the enemies using reflectors in order to work.

builds.f2p_weapons:
Nucleon + Quarker,
Subduer + Tamer,
or Decay + Blight
builds.f2p_drones:
Lv9 Hawkeye
Lv4 Showdown
builds.best_weapons:
Basilieus + Velos 
Pilum + Gladius or
Shantak + Voonith
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Hawkeye
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv12 Armadillo
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Phase Shift,
Repair, or UC
builds.explanation:
Siren is competent at midrange due to it's high battlefield visibility, strong reflector, and good
uptime. All these benefits come at the cost of firepower when compared to other midrangers,
but it's nonetheless worth it. Yang Lee pilot is generally not recommended due to Hawkeye 
being able to replace it so that you can have the 4 seconds of extra uptime from twins.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv9 Hawkeye
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Armadillo
Lv9 Whiteout
builds.best_weapons:
Liodari + Hippo or
Close range UE setups
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Hawkeye
Lv4 Showdown
Lv12 Armadillo
Lv9 Whiteout
builds.specialization:
Brawler (Repair Amp +
Immune Amp)
Jump Unit,
Repair, or UC
builds.explanation:
Raptor is a decent opening robot, regardless of if it's being used for titan charge or not. Jump unit
helps you reach center.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv9 Kestrel
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv1 Myia
builds.best_weapons:
Elox + Murix, Kroko +
Hippo, or UE burst
weapons
builds.specialization:
Support (Accelerator +
Last Stand)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Nightingale is a support/beacon capper hybrid with the potential to reach amazingly fast speeds.
Unlike Loki and Skyros, it lacks permanent protection, but it makes up for it with supportive utility.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv9 Kestrel
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv9 Armadillo
Lv9 Whteout
Lv9+ Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Quarker + Murix, Quarker
+ Kroko, Quarker +
Machiara, Fahrenheit +
Celsius, or close range
UE setups
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Webby
Lv12 Kestrel
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Dux weapons don't matter, you can even avoid the Quarker recommendation if you know what
you're doing and cycle your weapons such that the BiW fires infinitely. Dux drones' defense
mitigation allows them to kill tanky targets like Lio surprisingly fast.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv9 Kestrel
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv9 Armadilllo
Lv9 Whiteout
builds.best_weapons:
Elox + Murix,
UE Burst weapons,
or Kroko + Hippo
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Kestrel
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv12 Armadilllo
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv12 UE Glider
builds.specialization:
Any Specialization
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Scorpion should be played like a wuss, teleporting in to try and steal deft survivor triggers and
kills on squishy bots within it's short stealth duration and then immediately running away.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Boom + Bash + Pow
Hedjet + Deshret + Pshent,
Kroko + Hippo + Liodari or
Voonith + Urhag + Shantak
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Rangefind)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Kaji's tactical gameplay with its mines make it's own weapons relatively unimportant in the grand
scheme of things. Well placed mines can take down incredibly heavy and valuable targets, as well
as locking down important objectives. Rangefinder works regardless of weapons if mines are far.

builds.best_weapons: Shantak
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Rangefind)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Snipers are only good due to how OP Shantak + Solar is. If you don't have both of Shantak and
the Solar drone, it's hardly worth running a sniper. The alternative (Gauss) is pretty unimpressive
except for shutting down Lios. Not recommended for f2p.

builds.f2p_drones: Lv4+ Showdown
builds.best_weapons: Basilieus or Pilum
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Most gunboats are better relegated to being midrangers at f2p level investment. If you have
Piercers and Solar, however, you should just use that instead.

builds.f2p_weapons:
Devasator + Havoc
+ Scatter or Kirin +
Mogwan + Gangil
builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Armadillo
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Kroko + Hippo + Liodari or
Close range UE setups
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Scarebus
Lv4 Showdown
Lv12 Armadillo
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Brawler (Repair Amp +
Immune Amp)
Repair or UC
builds.explanation:
Teth is an effective brawler regardless of if you use it as a titan charger. It's EMP can interrput
other Brawlers like Rex and Ammit, and it's forcefield and regenerating physical shield lets it
sustain close ranged fights.

builds.f2p_weapons:
Claw + Jaw, Howler +
Growler, Mace + Cudgel,
or Atomizer + Quarker
builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Kroko + Hippo
Machiara + Velos
Close range UE setups
builds.specialization:
Tank (Repair Amp +
Heavy Armor Kit)
Repair or UC
builds.explanation:
Not much needs to be said about Ammit. It is the current broken robot, you can run almost
anything and it will probably work decently.

builds.f2p_weapons:
Hazard + Blight, Arms,
Damper + Tamer, or
Atomizer + Quarker
builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Machiara + Velos,
Midrange UE Setups
Voonith + Urhag
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Kestrel
Lv12 Showdown
Lv12 Aopo
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Phase Shift or
Jump Unit
builds.explanation:
Raven needs an optimal balance of range and damage to perform its primary duty, which is
shutting down enemy snipers and midrangers through massive mobility and extensive stealth.
As a result, its best weapons are midrange guns.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4+ showdown
Lv1 Whiteout
Lv1 Myia
builds.best_weapons: Doesn't matter
builds.best_drones:
Lv4+ showdown
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv12 Myia
builds.specialization:
Saboteur (Beacon Op +
Accelerator)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation: Similarly to Skyros, beacon capper Loki has pretty much no good reason to exit stealth.

builds.best_weapons: Elox
builds.specialization:
Saboteur (Beacon Op +
Accelerator)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Loki as an opportunist is just like Skyros as an opportunist, requiring very high burst damage
weapoms and a specific level 12 drone in order to perform.Not recommended for f2p.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Armadillo
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Murix, Kroko, Fahrenheit
or Close range UE setups
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Scales
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nike Amp + Overdrive)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Close ranged leech is a pretty dangerous build in 2026, but it's still viable if played with care. Thomas
mindread with engine expert is a must in order to get in and out dangerous situations. Scales drone
at high investment is also a huge upgrade, allowing leech to hold up in titan phase.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Armadillo
builds.best_weapons:
Midrange UE setups
Machiara or Urhag
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Scales
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Phase Shift
builds.explanation:
Midrange leech is a much safer leech variant. You should still ideally use Thomas with Engine expert.
but it's less important.

builds.best_weapons: Shantak
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Rangefind)
Phase Shift,
Repair, or UC
builds.explanation:
Snipers are only good due to how OP Shantak + Solar is. If you don't have both of Shantak and
the Solar drone, it's hardly worth running a sniper. The alternative (Gauss) is pretty unimpressive
except for shutting down Lios. Not recommended for f2p.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Armadillo
Lv4 Seeker
builds.best_weapons: Basilieus or Pilum
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Scales
Lv12 Armadillo
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Phase Shift,
Repair, or UC
builds.explanation:
Most gunboats are better relegated to being midrangers at f2p level investment. At non f2p investment,
If you don't have Basilieus but you do have Shantak/Piercer, use that instead.

builds.best_weapons:
Liodari, UE Redeemer,
UE Avenger
builds.best_drones:
Lv12 Scales
Lv12 Armadillo
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Phase Shift,
Repair, or UC
builds.explanation:
Bagliore as a Brawler is able to statcheck a surprising amount of enemies, especially if you properly
time the Nova Light healing and prepare groundwork in advance. This only works if you have high
quality weapons and mk3. Not recommended for f2p.

builds.f2p_weapons:
Damper + Tamer,
ARMS, or
Atomizer + Quarker
builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Armadillo
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv9 Aopo
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Midrange UE setups,
Machiara + Velos
builds.best_drones:
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv12 Armadillo
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv12 Aopo
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv12 WARD
Lv12 Seeker
builds.pilot:
Otto Schreyer
(With Arms)
Sam Stone
(With EMGS)
Shani Kyne
(if you have)
Miharu Sharpe
(Otherwise)
builds.specialization:
Raider (Nuke Amp +
Any)
Phase Shift or Lockdown
Ammo
builds.explanation:
Using Sword's Sword is no longer worth it. Sword should be played as though it were a sidegraded
Siren, teleporting back to the translocator to refresh the ability after the flight phase ends. It's very
much capable of outputting decent damage to enemies this way, though you may want to try using 
Lockdown Unit in order to ensure they can't escape you. While Sword doesn't suffer from Siren's issue
of getting bursted down by high damage weapons or titans, it is instead susceptable to shieldbreaker.

builds.best_weapons:
Elox + Murix, Hippo +
Kroko, Fahrenheit + 
Celsius, Close range UE
setups
builds.specialization:
Brawler (Repair Amp +
Immune Amp)
Repair or UC
builds.explanation:
Rex is able to deal unbelievably high damage for a brawler when equipped with the Danny pilot. The
reduced amount of charges gives it less uptime at the start of the battle, but it shouldn't matter
since you can end fights so quickly with the damage boost. Not recommended for f2p.

builds.f2p_weapons:
Decay + Blight,
Damper + Subduer, or
Nucleon + Quarker
builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Armadillo
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons:
Machiara + Velos
Pilum + Gladius or
Shantak + Voonith
builds.best_drones:
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv12 Armadillo
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv12 Seeker
builds.pilot:
Lucius Palma,
Lian Xin Bai
is competitive
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Phase Shift,
Repair, or UC
builds.explanation:
Hastatus is a solid midranger with a very powerful defensive ability. It's best played at a medium
range, and both of it's legendary pilots are quite strong. Webby does not work on Hastatus.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Armadillo
Lv9 Seeker
builds.best_weapons: Basilieus or Pilum
builds.best_drones:
Lv4+ Showdown
Lv12 Armadillo
Lv12 UE Glider
Lv12 Seeker
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Overdrive)
Phase Shift,
Repair, or UC
builds.explanation:
Most gunboats are better relegated to being midrangers at f2p level investment. At non f2p investment,
If you don't have Basilieus but you do have Shantak/Piercer, use that instead.

builds.best_weapons: Shantak
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke Amp + Rangefind)
Phase Shift,
Repair, or UC
builds.explanation:
Snipers are only good due to how powerful Shantak + Solar is. If you don't have both of Shantak and
the Solar drone, it's hardly worth running a sniper. The alternative (Gauss) is pretty unimpressive
except for shutting down Lios. Not recommended for f2p.

builds.f2p_weapons:
Damper + Subduer,
Screamer + Growler,
or Nucleon + Atomizer
builds.f2p_drones:
Lv9 Seeker
Lv9 Aopo
builds.best_weapons:
Machiara + Basilius or
UE Devastator + UE 
Havoc, UE Machineguns,
or Fahrenheit + Kelvin
builds.specialization:
Raider
(Nuke Amp + Integrated)
Phase Shift, SB, or QS
builds.explanation:
Ankasor will yield results as long as your screen is turned on while piloting it. This robot can
comfortably 1v1 most titans in the game with a bit of patience, it's only real counter is getting 
onetapped by croc guns or vendicatoires.

builds.best_weapons: Kroko + Liodari
builds.specialization:
Raider
(Nuke Amp + Integrated)
Phase Shift, SB, or QS
builds.explanation:
The idea behind this build is that you onetap the enemy before they onetap you. Also charges titans
absurdly fast, so it's a good opener

builds.f2p_weapons:
Howler + Growler,
Havoc + Scatter, or
Claw + Jaw
builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv9 Seeker
Lv9 Hawkeye
Lv9 Armadillo
builds.best_weapons:
Kroko + Liodari, Fahrenheit
+ Celsius, or close range
UE setups
builds.specialization:
Brawler (Repair Amp +
Immune Amp)
Phase Shift,
Repair, or UC
builds.explanation:
Vector is an absurdly fast brawler that can skate across the map. Regular brawling builds are very
strong on it, but as you might expect, fast brawlers tend to be best used as titan chargers.

builds.f2p_drones:
Lv4 Showdown
Lv9 Whiteout
Lv9 Seeker
Lv9 Hawkeye
builds.best_weapons: Kroko + Liodari
builds.specialization:
Brawler (Repair Amp +
Immune Amp)
Phase Shift or UC
builds.explanation:
Vector is probably the single best titan charging bot in the game, competing with the likes of UE
Brawlers and Teth.

builds.best_weapons: Urhag + Shantak
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke amp + Rangefind)
Phase shift or SB
builds.explanation:
A pathfinder with a nuclear amp is scary, but a pathfinder that can lock you down and turn you into
stacks from across the map is scarier.

builds.f2p_weapons:
Decay + Hazard
Nucleon + Atomizer
Subduer + Damper
builds.f2p_drones:
Lv9 Freezo
Lv9 Fhtagn
builds.best_weapons: Machiara + Basileus
builds.specialization:
Damage Dealer
(Nuke amp + Overdrive)
Phase shift or SB
builds.explanation: A more accessable support due to the lack of good f2p snipers in the meta.

robots.name: Harpy
robots.comments: Harpy's BiW blast effect provides a decent chunk of damage, but the weaker reflector holds it back from ever being in competetion with Siren.

robots.name: Siren
robots.comments: Between the twin fliers, Siren's freeze and stronger reflector give it a slight edge in supporting capability.

robots.name: Typhon
robots.comments: Typhon's access to mid-range support, availability in the super chest, and high damage make it a solid option. However, it is seriously outclassed by Pathfinder.

robots.name: Orochi
robots.comments: Orochi's multiple dashes with stealth are decent in a vacuum, but are also competing with Lynx and Raven.

robots.name: Demeter
robots.comments: Demeter's ability is possibly the strongest in the entire game, though on a moderate CD. Even at level 1, the absorber shield is hugely impactful.

robots.name: Jaeger
robots.comments: Jaeger is worse than Fury.

robots.name: Scorpion
robots.comments: Scorpion is good for picking off weakened or vulnerable enemies, even without much investment.

robots.name: Erebus
robots.comments: Erebus, similarly to Typhon, has solid mid-range support and excellent damage.

robots.name: Fafnir
robots.comments: While a lack of availability in the superchest and half-reliance on defense points to stay alive are unappealing, Fafnir's raw power and flexibility make up for it.

robots.name: Hawk
robots.comments: Compared to the other aerial bots, Hawk lacks both damage and utility. The retraction of weapons during flight really hurts it.

robots.name: Nightingale
robots.comments: A true support, Nightingale provides long-lasting healing and protection via its suppression effect at the cost of any offensive capacity.

robots.name: Ravana
robots.comments: Ravana is accessible, threatning, and nearly impossible to kill when Transcendance is used properly.

robots.name: Shell
robots.comments: Shell is a less accessible version of Hellburner that also runs the risk of charging opposing Nuclear Amplifiers.

robots.name: Khepri
robots.comments: Khepri has a solid mix of supporting and offensive capabilities, limited mainly by how modest both its base stats and its bonuses are individually.

robots.name: Revenant
robots.comments: Revenant's protection from grey damage necessitates tons of healing to make useful, which means lots of investment and a giant powercell burn rate.

robots.name: Skyros
robots.comments: Skyros is an F2P beacon capturing machine, not even requiring a single upgrade or any equipment to work.

robots.name: Seraph
robots.comments: Seraph technically has a niche via stellar air speed, but is otherwise unremarkable. It's alright for mindlessly clearing early PvE levels.

robots.name: Mars
robots.comments: Mars is heavily reliant on its turret to differentiate itself, but the slow projectile speed means it's unfortunately unreliable.

robots.name: Angler
robots.comments: Angler can provide minor team support and is somewhat hard to kill, but is also a slow tank.

robots.name: Nether
robots.comments: Nether's a decent beacon runner or suicide lead, but Imugi, Skyros, Loki, Raptor, and Teth all do the job better.

robots.name: Imugi
robots.comments: The cycle of stealth, healing, teleport+healing, repeat makes Imugi extremely powerful. With its availability in the workshop, it's one of the best F2P bots in the game.

robots.name: Lynx
robots.comments: Lynx's defensive tools in stealth and forcefield both don't care about level. That combined with the execute gun and high speed make Lynx a good hit-and-run damage dealer.

robots.name: Crisis
robots.comments: Near permanent stealth and long-range camping make Crisis a fairly safe sniper, but also can make it a liability on short maps with clumped beacons.

robots.name: Ophion
robots.comments: Ophion's built-in-weapon has only 350m range, forcing you to play on a knife's edge with the absorber.

robots.name: Ochokochi
robots.comments: Compared to Fenrir, Ocho does have some utility and mobility, but is similarly hard to invest in. It's best saved for the few maps where it can knock people off edges.

robots.name: Dagon
robots.comments: Dagon is moderately powerful but difficult to invest in, given its stat-check nature and 6 hardpoints.

robots.name: Curie
robots.comments: Curie's an all-around decent bot, but is short-ranged and thus a less reliable lockdown source than Typhon, Erebus, Siren, or Pathfinder.

robots.name: Shenlou
robots.comments: Shenlou is reliant on its dome Aegis for protection, which causes it to get into more stat-checking scenarios than Scorpion's stealth.

robots.name: Pathfinder
robots.comments: Pathfinder's performance remains incredible across all investment levels; between speed boosts, damage boosts, lockdown, shieldbreak, healing, and more, it can truly do it all.

robots.name: Raptor
robots.comments: Raptor's jumps provide it with just enough flexibility to act as an off-tank beacon runner in a role similar to Leech or Teth.

robots.name: Bagliore
robots.comments: Bagliore hits harder than Crisis, is bulkier, and has a teleport, but has no stealth, power stored in a MK3 upgrade, and no built-in shieldbreaker.

robots.name: Condor
robots.comments: Condor's a mediore statchecker with no notable traits that'd make it worth investing into.

robots.name: Dux
robots.comments: Dux has high damage output and decent survivability, making it a solid tank-buster similar to Lynx.

robots.name: Ravager
robots.comments: Ravager is functionally a bulkier Crisis without the shieldbreaker. However, 5 hardpoints make it hard to invest in. It can alternatively be a slower Loki.

robots.name: Samjok
robots.comments: Samjok is easier to take down between phases compared to ravana. The turret is still deadly, even post nerf, though phase uptime is severely limited.

robots.name: Nuo
robots.comments: Nuo's damage and teammate-buffing abilities are strong. Though not listed ingame, it also gets tremendous in-flight speed.

robots.name: Lio
robots.comments: Lio's bulk and teammate-buffing abilities are strong.

robots.name: Sword Unit
robots.comments: Sword has been downgraded to an adjacent-siren since its release, but still remains as a satisfactory damage dealer & tank buster. Lockdown Unit is a great way to keep a tank in place and soften them up for your teammates.

robots.name: Teth
robots.comments: Teth is an extremely mobile tank with good utility, making it able to do Raptor's off-tank beacon runner role very effectively.

robots.name: Stryx
robots.comments: Stryx has incredibly high fire power. However, 5 hard points make it difficult to invest in.

robots.name: Kaji
robots.comments: Kaji is a strategic minelayer with the potential to tear high health & slower enemies apart. It doesn't rely on its weapons much, having high damage potential with the mines alone in most cases.

robots.name: Rex
robots.comments: Rex is the second coming of Ochokochi. Very strong, but a bit difficult to invest in. Aside from being a newer robot and thus locked to more expensive pads, it suffers if not mk3 and outfitted with more complete features (drone, pilot, etc).

robots.name: Hastatus
robots.comments: Hastatus is a versatile midranger with an OP new defense system that can be best described as "Phase shift but you can shoot." Very strong but somewhat difficult to invest in, requiring mk3 for maximum efficiency.

robots.name: Ammit
robots.comments: Ammit does too much. It has the ability of a glass cannon with the stats of a tank, all while passively applying negative effects to anyone nearby. While it's already OP at base, it can be even further improved, as investing in this robot makes it pretty much gamebreaking.

robots.name: Anaksor
robots.comments: Anaksor is an exceptional assassin and tank-buster. Utility, mobility, and evasiveness make it great even when just MK2'd.

robots.name: Vector
robots.comments: Vector is a modern cross between an Ochokochi and a Raptor: It has both immense survivability and map-crossing speed. Unfortunately, it's not any easier to invest in than other new brawlers, requiring every piece of the puzzle and mk3 for maximum impact.

robots.name: Shoggoth
robots.comments: It was hard to believe that pixonic would powercreep Pathfinder, but you truly can't underestimate their motives to add the most unhealthy robots to the game as possible. Shoggoth is an unholy abomination child between mother Pathfinder and father Bagliore.

robots.name: Fury
robots.comments: Fury has decent firepower, comes pre-leveled, and its pilot grants it access to unlimited shieldbreaker. It's a passable sniper to use as a weapon platform while you wait for something better.

robots.name: Strider
robots.comments: Strider has some potential with the legendary pilot, but it is too outclassed by Nether.

robots.name: Raven
robots.comments: Raven is the gold standard for offensive F2P bots, staying viable all the way up in championship league via its legendary pilot.

robots.name: Rayker
robots.comments: Rayker's long-range support ability makes it a tenable contributor to a team, even with no investment.

robots.name: Ao Guang
robots.comments: Ao Guang is mediocre in the air and terrible outside of it.

robots.name: Bulgasari
robots.comments: Bulgasari is mediocre to bad across the board, and really needs its legendary pilot to function at all.

robots.name: Hellburner
robots.comments: With the legendary pilot, Hellburner becomes a kamikaze machine, but it is limited by low durability and terrible hardpoints.

robots.name: Cerberus
robots.comments: Cerberus's utility via Blackout is seriously limited by its low range and lack of an EMP.

robots.name: Haechi
robots.comments: Haechi is similar to Bulgasari, but also lacks a decent pilot.

robots.name: Inquisitor
robots.comments: Inquisitor does a decent Raven impression early on, but once Raven gets its legendary pilot, Inquisitor is strictly worse.

robots.name: Falcon
robots.comments: Falcon has all the tools it needs to succeed, but it can't use them all at the same time. T-falcon is very cheap, but falls off very quickly.

robots.name: Pursuer
robots.comments: The existence of Loki means that Pursuer needs to make use of its ability to fire while stealthed. Unfortunately, 3 lights and no dash means it can't reliably assassinate.

robots.name: Spectre
robots.comments: Spectre is one of multiple Inquisitor sidegrades that all fall short of Raven. 4M makes it the best sidegrade, but still not good enough.

robots.name: Mercury
robots.comments: Mercury is a sidegrade to Inquisitor, trading hardpoint strength for a better ability.

robots.name: Nemesis
robots.comments: Nemesis sacrifices too much firepower and speed for its absorber shield.

robots.name: Weyland
robots.comments: Weyland's an extremely slow close-range support, necessitating heavy investment for not enough return.

robots.name: Behemoth
robots.comments: Behemoth's worse than Bagliore in basically every way. Even Fury has a niche over it with its shieldbreaker.

robots.name: Bulwark
robots.comments: Bulwark is a statcheck machine that loses to other statcheck machines.

robots.name: Invader
robots.comments: Invader's biggest issue is that as a statchecking tank, you'll need to upgrade it tremendously before it will have enough durability to do its job.

robots.name: Mender
robots.comments: While like Weyland, Mender is a close-range support, its emphasis on speed gives it an edge.

robots.name: Loki
robots.comments: Loki's blazing fast speed and permanent stealth make it an excellent beacon runner, even with no investment required.

robots.name: Tyr
robots.comments: Tyr's an unimpressive middle ground between Weyland's bulk and Mender's speed.

robots.name: Blitz
robots.comments: Blitz plays a lot like Hellburner, but has the added bonuses of a longer ability and access to suppression.

robots.name: Fenrir
robots.comments: Fenrir is a strange case. It's free and can use midrange weapons somewhat well, but reaching its maximum potential as a closerange statchecker requires tons of investment.

robots.name: Hades
robots.comments: Hades is better than Nemesis, but is still plainly outclassed by other absorber bots.

robots.name: Ao Jun
robots.comments: Ao Jun is frail and requires getting extremely close in order to use its flamethrowers, making it too risky for not much reward. It's short uptime ability and long cooldown make it extremely clunky, especially in comparison to other flying bots.

robots.name: Ares
robots.comments: With high damage, Ares has the power to accomplish a decent amount during its absorber uptime.

robots.name: Leech
robots.comments: Leech is a huge threat on the battlefield with proper positioning and timing of its ability. After its shift from T4 to T3, it's also extremely accessible.

robots.name: Phantom
robots.comments: Phantom has to compete with Hellburner, Leech, and Blitz. While the teleporter is nice, it's worse in every other aspect.

titans.name: Kid
titans.comments: Kid is given for free. It's only worth using until you get any other titan to replace it with.

titans.name: Arthur
titans.comments: T2 titans are not worth wasting platinum on. Their lack of specializations neuters their stats.

titans.name: Ao Ming
titans.comments: T2 titans are not worth wasting platinum on. Their lack of specializations neuters their stats.

titans.name: Nodens
titans.comments: Nodens is a cheap, simple, and reasonably effective support. The increased defense mitigation it has on its passive makes it no slouch on offense despite having only beta weapons.

titans.name: Minos
titans.comments: Minos is a fast, safe, and cheap beacon runner, though it'll struggle to kill titans due to its lack of Alpha hardpoints.

titans.name: Sharanga
titans.comments: Sharanga is an offensive titan without the proper tools to stay safe or gain target access.

titans.name: Murometz
titans.comments: Murometz can often straight-up kill opposing titans while they are EMP'd by it, making it a solid, cheap anti-titan titan.

titans.name: Luchador
titans.comments: Luchador is one of the weaker brawling titans, making it a statchecker that is bad at statchecking.

titans.name: Heimdall
titans.comments: Heimdall has decent supporting capacity, though its extra cost over Nodens comes with questionable benefit.

titans.name: Indra
titans.comments: Like Ravana, Indra's phasing and lasso scale with the opponent's strength, making it an excellent anti-meta choice.

titans.name: Aether
titans.comments: Aether is to Murometz what Heimdall is to Nodens. More expensive with questionable sidegrades.

titans.name: Sirius
titans.comments: With a giant hitbox, poor speed, and miniscule damage, Sirius is a sitting duck.

titans.name: Rook
titans.comments: Rook's huge dash lets it play the role of an assassin or beacon runner, though it does the job very unreliably and lacks good survival tools.

titans.name: Newton
titans.comments: Newton's a solid sniper whose choke provides good team utility.

titans.name: Eiffel
titans.comments: If you can afford to build it by acquiring weapons for and leveling the required 5 hardpoints, Eiffel has unmatched raw firepower. Hybrid setups using instant unload snipers (ie Vendicatoire) in the summoned slots are recommended.

titans.name: Bedwyr
titans.comments: Bedwyr's taunt and giant absorber provide great team support and survivability that doesn't depend solely on stats, respectively.

titans.name: Bersagliere
titans.comments: Between stealth, 3 Alphas, and a large jump, Bersagliere has all the tools it needs to be a strong, safe damage dealer.

titans.name: Mauler
titans.comments: Mauler is extremely deadly, and enemies in its abillity's zone will melt rapidly. However, without significant investment, it will be frail for a long time.

titans.name: Devourer
titans.comments: Devourer is a debuffer that can seriously impede enemies' movement, damage, and defense by essence shifting them. Unfortunately, it relies on allies or mothership effects in order to make use of this ability.

titans.name: Bjorn
titans.comments: Bjorn is a decent statchecker with high uptime and firepower but poor mobility outside of ball mode. It trades luchador's jump for it's better ability uptime and an extra alpha weapon.

titans.name: Atlas
titans.comments: Atlas is comparable to Bedwyr, namely trading off Taunt and a more utility focused absorber shield for nasty EMP application & area denial, plus some mobility.

titans.name: Princeps
titans.comments: Princeps has an overpowered mix of high damage, the best defensive ability in the game, and as a cherry on top, crowd control utility.

titans.name: Waymaker
titans.comments: Waymaker is a potent support titan that's dragged down by pixonic gimping much of its kit to overcompensate for how strong hunter stacks are. It also suffers from opportunity cost; pathfinder only requires a robot slot to do what it does while waymaker requires a titan slot.

camelot.name: Lancelot
camelot.description:
Lancelot is limited to 1x for good reasons. It has no discernable weakness; it is fast, bulky, and hard-hitting, all at the same time. A peak speed of 58 kph, 309k in physical shields, and 1H+2M hardpoints
are already each bot-defining characteristics on their own; putting them together creates a bot that is simply unmatched in combat versatility.

camelot.name: Fury
camelot.description:
Fury is also limited to 1x. However, unlike Lancelot, it has a clear weakness; its lack of L/M hardpoints make it vulnerable to energy damage. It is also far slower. Still, 3H is nearly unmatched firepower,
and its top-mounted hardpoints give it an advantage when peeking over the top of ledges and walls. These two features make it a great candidate for a DPS bot or Sniper.

camelot.name: Natasha
camelot.description:
Natasha has a lot of similarities to Fury, most notably the high firepower and slow movespeed. However, the exchange of 1H for 2L hardpoints makes a big difference. While Natasha is slightly worse
at peeking ledges, it is slightly better at peeking corners. More critically, 2L gives it the option to fit on an Ecu, allowing it to bypass Fury's energy weakness and act as a brawler like Lancelot.

camelot.name: Griffin
camelot.description:
Griffin's jump gives it target access that no bot other than Rogatka can replicate. 2M+2L firepower is also above average. Griffin's one weakness is that it has no defensive options whatsoever, as trying
to equip an Ecu will result in too much DPS loss. The fact that its 2M slots are on the side, as a result, hinders it defensively. That said, it competes with Fury for role of best damage dealer.

camelot.name: Rhino
camelot.description:
With the option to have permanently doubled speed as well as 226k in physical shields, Rhino is the premier beacon runner of the format. In addition, 2M+2L is solid firepower, and the ability to
safely move under Lancelot's Ancile and unload Orkans on it lets it consistently counter the only S-tier bot. Its weaknesses are a high skill-cap, which can be overcome, and rockets, which are rare.

camelot.name: Raijin
camelot.description:
Raijin is an absolute beast of a bot when in stationary mode with 338k in physical shields, as well as a 40% damage boost. That said, that's the exact problem with it; it has to be stationary, making it very
vulnerable to sniping, splash, and corner-peeking. It still has a decent number of options, though it competes somewhat unfavorably with Natasha and Fury on each.

camelot.name: Butch
camelot.description:
Butch's frailty and lack of ability to equip Ancile make it incapable of brawling, but the fact that it technically has 4H firepower gives it potential to be the best sniper in certain situations. Trebuchet works
best with its cooldowns, but other sniping weapons can work too.

camelot.name: Galahad
camelot.description:
Galahad is a fair version of Lancelot. It has weaker shields, less in-combat speed, and less firepower than the titan of the format. Despite this, Galahad carves out a niche due to the mere presence of its
physical shield and the fact that Lancelot is limited to 1x. This makes it both a generalist and a good tech option in energy-heavy games, where it can 1v1 any bots that aren't Ancilot or Ancilot cosplayers.

camelot.name: Leo
camelot.description:
Leo's builds are all worse versions of Lancelot, Natasha, and Fury. It can't run both Ancile and Ecu, since that limits its firepower to 2L. That said, its high raw health means that it can ditch Ecu altogether.
This makes it appealing in metas with primarily physical and rocket weapons, where its Ancile builds in particular can be decent generalist brawlers.

camelot.name: Fujin
camelot.description:
In metas without energy weapons, Fujin's 126k constantly-regenerating energy shield is even bigger than that of Ancile. 3M is also no joke in terms of firepower. However, Fujin's slowness and frailty
make it free target practice for most snipers while also making it harder to reposition in a brawl. Ultimately, it largely competes unfavorably with Natasha, but it can do a decent Fury impression.

camelot.name: Stalker
camelot.description:
Stalker is fast and has access to the only stealth in the format, making it a reasonable beacon runner. Depending on equipment, it's also good at finishing off enemies with even the slightest bit of chip.
However, stealth is not invulnerability, and the Stalker player will need to be very careful with their positioning to not get hit. Rhino is also simply a faster runner.

camelot.name: Rogatka
camelot.description:
Rogatka's frequent jumping allows it to carve a niche as a bot with even more target access than Griffin. A speed of 60 kph is also solid. However, 2M hardpoints and 125k durability combine to make a bot
that cannot reliably duel any other. Rogatka therefore relies on surprising weakened players via high-burst weapons.
Builds:

camelot.name: Bolt
camelot.description:
Bolt is the only bot in the format with access to a dash. This strength "combines well" with Bolt's weaknesses of extreme frailty and a paltry 3L hardpoints to force it into a niche of being a hit-and-run bot.
In most cases, you'll want to dash into severely weakened opponents to finish them off with burst. If you opt to use Aphids, you'll have the additional option of firing at 350m and dashing away to safety.
Builds:

camelot.name: Carnage
camelot.description:
At first glance, Carnage might be appealing. Two heavies, a speed boost, and a built-in energy shield are useful traits. However, its shield is weaker than an Ancile's and Carnage's hull durability is barely
above 100k, making it particularly vulnerable to energy weapons. This means that it is mostly outclassed in its Avenger role by Fury and its midrange role by Fujin, leaving only Thunders as a true niche.
Builds:

camelot.name: Gl. Patton
camelot.description:
In terms of hardpoints and durability, Gl. Patton is strictly outdone by Griffin, who also additionally has the ability to jump. Gl. Patton's extra 2 kph are nowhere near enough to offset this. In light of that,
why is Gl. Patton here? The answer lies in the one light weapon for which there is no medium counterpart: Gekko Aphid. Aphid can be decent against inexperienced players on some maps, such as Moon.
Builds:

camelot.name: Jesse
camelot.description:
Jesse might initially appear to be even worse off than Gl. Patton is. Only two of its light hardpoints are active at any time, and the switch is gated by a 10 second cooldown. Jesse is also far frailer.
However, Aphid happens to also have a cooldown of 10s, making the Aphid build's output similar to that of Gl. Patton. Although Jesse is still frailer, it's also far faster, boasting 64 kph.
Builds:

camelot.name: Gareth
camelot.description:
Gareth takes all the disadvantages Galahad has and amplifies them to the next level, with even less firepower and an even lower overall durability. Despite this, it still can find itself a niche. Its shield is
just about twice its raw durability, giving it surprising bulk against energy weapons, and it packs even more speed, giving it the smallest of niches as a beacon runner.
Builds:

camelot.name: Doc
camelot.description:
Doc, unlike Jesse, lacks an Aphid-like weapon to take a niche from Butch with. However, its decent speed of 51 and slightly faster quick-draw cooldown than Butch let it opt for speed and flexibility while
still having similarly high burst potential. Thus, it can run Ion, which deals almost the same damage as Zeus, and/or Tulumbas, which unloads quicker and thus has higher burst DPS than Trident.
Builds:

camelot.name: Gepard
camelot.description: Bolt's dash is far more relevant than Gepard's technically higher stats. Ditto Gl. Patton's fourth light slot.

camelot.name: Schutze
camelot.description: Old "Removed" bot. 1H is simply pitiful firepower.

camelot.name: Destrier
camelot.description: Strictly outclassed even by its fellow F-tiers.

camelot.name: Cossack
camelot.description: While the constant jumping might seem like it could facilitate a beacon running strategy, Cossack's 65k durability just means it dies to everything. Rogatka isn't great, but still does it better.

camelot.name: Boa
camelot.description: Old "Removed" bot. Slow bots need to be both bulky and powerful. Boa fits the former, but not the latter, and is thus outclassed by Leo.

camelot.name: Vityaz
camelot.description: While it's designed for cornershooting its 1H, cornershooting is a nearly universal mechanic. Vityaz is clearly outgunned and out-bulked by Natasha and Leo, respectively.

camelot.name: Golem
camelot.description: Old "Removed" bot. This gets the closest to competing with Leo, but still is definitively worse.

footnotes: ***Only with Lock-down Ammo module or Paralysis drone
